Assembly: The assembly fDipBim1.hap1.1 is based on 25x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A heterozygous chromosomal fission was observed on chromosome 1.Complex heterozygous reciprocal translocations and associated rearrangements were observed between chromosomes 10 and 12.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP1 chromosome 12 (69,200 - 72,900 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP2 chromosome 12 are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP1 chromosome 13 (79,700 - 83,700 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly of Hexaplex trunculus (xgHexTrun1) is based on 52X ONT data and 136X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 27 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 14.372 Mb (with the largest being 10.011 Mb). Additionally, 251 regions totaling 68.164 Mb (with the largest being 4.783 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 11 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 27.8Mb (with the largest being 9.3Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly fDipBim1.hap2.1 is based on 25x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A heterozygous chromosomal fission was observed on chromosome 1.Complex heterozygous reciprocal translocations and associated rearrangements were observed between chromosomes 10 and 12.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP1 chromosome 12 (69,200 - 72,900 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP2 chromosome 12 are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP1 chromosome 13 (79,700 - 83,700 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly wcApoIcte1.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly wcApoCali1.hap1.1 is based on 48x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of contigs along chromosome 10 from 45.8 Mb to the end is uncertain. Chromosome 12 has a heterozygous inversion between 18.6 Mb and 22.8 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly ilHydRube1.hap1.1 is based on 43x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal.
